article1.title: Signing of documents for accreditation
article1.content:
"This is a memorable day for the Mama Esther association. Today, the creation of the association was officially recognized by the Thionville court in Moselle, marking the beginning of a new chapter in the mission of this philanthropic organization.

 The members of the association and supporters had been waiting impatiently for this news for months. When the official documents arrived by mail, a wave of joy and relief swept through the team. The president of the association, Esther Gérard, wanted to announce the good news to all members and volunteers. 

We have worked tirelessly to reach this moment, declared Esther Gérard, visibly moved. Receiving these official documents is a recognition of our commitment and our efforts. This is the beginning of an adventure full of promise for the children we support. 

The signing of the documents by the Thionville court symbolizes much more than a simple legal formality. It is the result of years of hard work, dedication and unconditional support from many volunteers and supporters. Each member of the association contributed in their own way to this achievement. 

The receipt of the official documents marks the beginning of a new journey for the Mama Esther association, a journey filled with compassion, determination and hope. Each member of the association is now ready to roll up their sleeves and transform this vision into reality, offering a better future to children and vulnerable people. 

the Webmaster"

article2.title: Visit to the association's land
article2.content:
"The sun shone gently over the vast land, highlighting the enthusiasm and hope that permeated the air. Today, a crucial step in the Mama Esther association's project was about to take shape. The president of the association, Mrs. Esther GERARD, was on site to visit the land intended to host the future orphanage building, a project that promises to transform many lives. The land, currently a vast and open stretch of ground, was about to become a haven for children in need. The Mama Esther association, known for its commitment to the well-being of orphaned children and vulnerable people, had worked tirelessly to begin the construction of this new facility. 

While walking across the field, the president took the time to discuss with members of the local community, listening to their ideas and suggestions with benevolent attention. 'This project is not only that of the association 'Mama Esther' she declared. It belongs to each of us. Together, we are building a better future for these children, offering them not only a roof, but also a home where they can thrive. 

The president of the 'Mama Esther' association left the field with a heart filled with hope and enthusiasm, ready to continue working tirelessly to make this dream a reality. Today's visit was much more than a simple inspection; it symbolized the realization of a vision, carried by a community united by a common goal: to offer a safe and loving home to the children who need it most. 

The Webmaster"

article3.title: Preparation of the Concrete Blocks!
article3.content:
On the association's land, the concrete blocks used for the constructions do not come from distant factories: they are manufactured directly on site by local entrepreneurs. These artisans prepare the mixture, mold each block by hand and ensure careful drying. This choice allows not only to guarantee materials adapted to the terrain, but also to involve local businesses, an essential commitment to support the village economy and strengthen links with the community.
Once dry, the blocks are stored in the family house, where they remain protected from humidity and bad weather. Methodically arranged, they are ready to be transported to the site at the time of the work. This simple and efficient organization ensures the quality of the materials and contributes to the good progress of the construction projects.

article6.title: Laying of the first stone
article6.content: The foundations are completed. The laying of the first stone was carried out by our big sister who is also our project manager, helped by our big brother who is also our construction administrator, marking the official start of the construction work for this building which is intended to be first an orphanage, and also a safe and warm refuge for people in great difficulty. It is a symbolic step that brings us closer to our goal of providing a loving home and education opportunities for vulnerable children in the region. We are eager to see this project come to life and to continue working to improve the lives of these children and the people who will need it.

article4.title: Felling of the trees!
article4.content:
While the breeze blocks are manufactured on the land, a local company intervenes to fell the trees present on the construction zone. The wood is then cut into planks directly on site, in order to be used to create the formwork necessary for the elevation of the walls. This step allows to transform a resource already available on the land into an essential material for the site.
This approach is part of a logic of reuse and economy of means. By using wood from the site, the association limits external purchases, reduces construction costs and avoids waste. The wood that is not used for the formwork is not lost: it is recovered by the villagers, who need it for cooking food, which further reinforces the social utility of this local resource.
Beyond the practical aspect, this approach also reinforces the local anchoring of the site: the resources of the land are used intelligently, materials are recycled on site, and the village economy is supported through the intervention of local companies.

article5.title: Delimitation of the land
article5.content:
Once the tree felling is finished and the wood valued, the team moves on to an essential step: the precise delimitation of the land. The contours of the future house are traced on the ground, which allows to define the exact location of the load-bearing walls and the areas to be dug. These landmarks serve as a guide to organize the space and prepare the digging of the foundations.
While this preparation is in progress, another team of the company manufactures the iron frames by hand.
This simultaneous organization — tracing of the ground on one side, manufacturing of the frames on the other — allows to move forward efficiently and prepare all the necessary elements before the start of the construction itself. It also illustrates the capacity of local teams to optimize time and available resources.

article7.title: Elevation of the walls
article7.content:
After several weeks of preparation — tree felling, wood valuation, terrain delimitation and framework manufacturing — the construction site enters a particularly visible phase: the elevation of the walls. The teams begin to arrange the breeze blocks manufactured on site, row after row, rising little by little. The team's work is progressing well.
This step marks a turning point in the project: the house is finally starting to take shape, the volumes are emerging, and the families can concretely visualize the progress of their future home. It is a strong moment on the field, where we see the result of collective work materializing day after day.

article8.title: The last rubble stones!
article8.content: With the elevation of the walls now well advanced, the construction site enters its final phase. The teams focus on the finishing of the walls, rectifying irregularities and manufacturing the last rubble stones. These two operations carried out simultaneously testify to the methodical approach of the project: every detail is finalized with care to ensure a solid, clean delivery that meets expectations. This step allows us to orient ourselves for the following work: the laying of the roof! But we will see that starting next spring, if everything works as planned!

aboutSection.title: Who are we?
aboutSection.description1: The Mama Esther Association works for the well-being of orphans and people in difficulty. Our mission is to help the most vulnerable: children without support, isolated elderly people, individuals with disabilities, destitute families.
aboutSection.description2: Thanks to the commitment of our members and volunteers, we implement solidarity actions, personalized support and human and material support projects. Together, we work for a more just and caring world.

transparency.title: Transparent accounting
transparency.text: The Mama Esther Association ensures that it maintains rigorous and transparent accounting. We use advanced financial management systems to ensure that all transactions are accurately recorded. Every donation and every expense is meticulously tracked and regularly audited to ensure an ethical and effective use of resources. Our commitment to flawless accounting reflects our dedication to transparency and accountability to our donors and beneficiaries. By ensuring rigorous financial management, we strengthen the trust of our donors and partners and ensure that every contribution is used to maximize the impact of our actions.

support.title: Support for children but also those in need
support.text: The Mama Esther Association is committed to providing global support to the community, starting with children but not limited to them. We offer services including: tutoring, medical care and moral support, to ensure their well-being. But it doesn't stop there. We extend our efforts to adults in difficulty, providing professional training and access to essential resources. By taking a holistic approach, we aim to strengthen the community as a whole, creating an environment where everyone can thrive. Our goal is to build a better future for everyone, for the youngest while supporting the adults who play a crucial role in their development.

dedication.title: Dedication without expectation
dedication.text: The Mama Esther Association relies on the unwavering commitment of its volunteers, whose selfless volunteering is the key to our success. Every day, they offer their time and energy without expecting anything in return, driven by the desire to make a difference in the lives of the most vulnerable. Their dedication goes far beyond simple tasks, as they provide support, hope and comfort to those who need it most. This volunteering, guided by values of solidarity and compassion, is essential to carry out our missions. Thanks to these everyday heroes, we are able to achieve ambitious projects and provide precious help to our community. Their altruism and generosity bear witness to their true spirit.

team.title: OUR TEAM
team.hint: Click on an image to learn more

descriptions.esther: My name is Esther GERARD, a nursing assistant working in Luxembourg. For decades, I have had it in my heart to create an association to take care of the children of Cameroon, my native country, as well as those in need. These children must find values in their lives and understand that the misfortune that touches them is neither a fatality nor an end. Like a mother, I feel this deep need to offer them the love of a mother they no longer have. We also extend this love to disadvantaged people and those in need. I thus wish to bring my skills and knowledge to those who need it most because for them too, the misfortune that touches them is neither a fatality nor an end.
descriptions.florent: My name is Florent GERARD and I am the husband of 'Mama Esther'. The idea of creating an association to help underprivileged children was born from a common dream. I have always loved children, innocent victims of a world often indifferent to their fate. Although some people make efforts, the means are not always sufficient and, despite the care provided, the children do not receive what they deserve. In addition to helping children, we also help disadvantaged people. A civil servant in France for 30 years, I manage all the administration of the association and supervise the site and social networks in order to contribute my skills and knowledge.
descriptions.maeva: My name is Maryam, treasurer of the association and daughter of our president, 'Mama Esther'. From a very young age, I was inspired by my mother's commitment and passion for helping underprivileged children. Having grown up with these values, I decided to fully invest myself in this noble cause. As treasurer, I ensure the good financial management of the association, in order to guarantee that every donation is used effectively and transparently. My goal is to contribute to offering a better future to children and people in need, by bringing my dedication to our common mission. I am convinced that, through our collective commitment, we can bring about significant and lasting changes.
descriptions.aziz: My name is Aziz and I am the president of the NGO AVUCA (aid to the Vulnerable of Central Africa). For several years, I have had the honor of working to improve their living conditions. Being an orphan of father and mother myself, I deeply understand the challenges that vulnerable children face. It is with enthusiasm that I commit myself within the 'Mama Esther' association. I am eager to share my experience and skills to provide concrete support to these children, learning from other members of this beautiful community. I firmly believe that, together, we can transform their lives and offer them a better future. Thank you for welcoming me among you.
descriptions.margault: My name is Margault, I am 29 years old and I come from Belgium. A nurse by profession, I am passionate about my work and the well-being of others. Outside of my profession, I am very athletic and I involve myself in activities that allow me to stay fit and balanced. I love traveling, discovering new cultures, and meeting people from all walks of life. Animals also have an important place in my life. Finally, yoga is a practice that I particularly appreciate for its soothing and revitalizing side. My commitment to the 'Mama Esther' association is a way for me to contribute to a cause that is close to my heart and to provide my help to those who need it most.
descriptions.marie: Hello, my name is Marie, I am 24 years old and I am a home nurse specialized in children. Passionate about travel, human encounters and volunteering abroad, I wish to transmit my values and skills to children and local populations. My goal is to improve the living conditions of Cameroonian communities through my relational and professional skills, and my passion for solidarity and humanity. I believe that every small action can have a big impact. My commitment to the 'Mama Esther' association aims to contribute to concrete projects having a real impact on the ground. Looking forward to receiving your donations, discussing with you or meeting you. 🙂
descriptions.melanie: My name is Melanie, a nurse working in Luxembourg. When my colleague, Esther, told me about the creation of an association to help children in Cameroon, I did not hesitate for a second to bring my dedication to this extraordinary humanitarian journey. Supporting these children in their well-being, education, development, and health is one of my priorities. By contributing to this cause, I hope to inspire others to participate in humanitarian initiatives and make a real difference in the lives of children. Together, we can create a better future for these children, and I look forward to starting this human and solidarity adventure.

edition.title: SITE EDITION
edition.text: The edition and management of the Site's publication is handled by Mr. Florent GERARD, residing at 1 Rue des Troènes, with the telephone number 06 45 65 65 17, and the email address florent.gerard@mamaesther.org. Hereafter the Publisher.

host.title: HOST
host.text: The Site's host is the company LWS - 007 Hébergement, with its registered office located at 10 rue Penthièvre 75008 Paris.

access.title: SITE ACCESS
access.text: The Site is normally accessible to the User at any time. However, the Publisher may, at any time, suspend, limit or interrupt the Site in order to carry out updates or modifications to its content. The Publisher shall in no case be held responsible for the possible consequences of this unavailability on the User's activities.

property.title: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AND COUNTERFEITING
property.text: The Mama Esther Association owns the intellectual property rights or holds the usage rights for all elements accessible on the site, notably: texts, images, graphics, logo, icons. Any reproduction, representation, modification, publication, adaptation of all or part of the elements of the site, whatever the means or process used, any unauthorized exploitation of the site or any element it contains will be considered as constituting an infringement and prosecuted in accordance with the provisions of articles L.335-2 and following of the Intellectual Property Code, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Mama Esther Association.

cookies.title: COOKIES AND TRACKERS
cookies.text: The Mama Esther Association website does not have an audience measurement system or social network sharing functions. You will therefore not be asked to accept cookies or other trackers. However, you have the possibility to click on the links to our social networks. The Mama Esther Association cannot be held responsible for the consequences of your navigation on these social networks.

contact.title: CONTACT FORM AND COMMENTS
contact.text: You may be required to provide us with your email address when you fill out our contact form or post a comment on one of the site's articles. In no case will your data be transferred to third parties. We expressly insist on the respect of your data.

newsletter.title: NEWSLETTER
newsletter.text: You can subscribe to the site's newsletter. You then automatically and free of charge receive newsletters dealing with topics from the Mama Esther Association site. You can unsubscribe at any time by clicking on the unsubscribe link at the bottom of each newsletter. In no case will your email address be transferred to third parties.

links.title: HYPERLINKS
links.text: This website contains a number of hyperlinks to other sites. However, the Mama Esther Association does not have the possibility to follow and verify the content of these sites, and will consequently assume no responsibility for this fact.

rss.title: RSS FEEDS
rss.text: RSS feeds are exclusively intended for site visitors for personal use and should in no case be used to feed other sites, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Mama Esther Association.

privacy.title: PERSONAL DATA PROTECTION
privacy.text: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Mama Esther Association is committed to guaranteeing the confidentiality and security of the personal data collected. This data is used exclusively within the framework of the association's activities (donations, contact, volunteering...) and is never transferred or sold to third parties. Each user has a right of access, rectification, opposition and deletion of their personal data, which they can exercise by simple request to the following address:

signature.title: ASSOCIATIVE SIGNATURE
signature.text: The Mama Esther Association is committed to welcoming your data as it welcomes its beneficiaries: with respect, security and benevolence.

hero.mainTitle: Building a dignified future for orphans and vulnerable people in
hero.subtext: We transform your donations into concrete actions: education, health and infrastructure for the most vulnerable children.

missions.subtitle: Our areas of action
missions.title: How we act daily
missions.eduDesc: The fulfillment of each person guides our commitment through adapted and benevolent support.
missions.eduFull: The fulfillment of each person guides our commitment. We ensure to offer children, women victims of violence and vulnerable people an environment that fosters their personal development and reconstruction. We finance the uniforms, books and school supplies necessary for the children and we provide support adapted to the needs of women victims of violence as well as vulnerable people. Our goal is to give them the essential means to find stability, confidence and a serene future.
missions.infraDesc: Buildings up to standards, access to drinking water, and creation of healthy and secure living spaces.
missions.infraFull: A safe and well-maintained environment is essential to protect the children and residents to foster their development. The association's building was designed to respect the latest legal standards in infrastructure and safety. Access to drinking water in the building is guaranteed thanks to the drilling of a secure well, allowing sustainable autonomy. Each stone laid is a brick towards a more dignified and secure life.
missions.transDesc: Priority support for orphans and accompaniment of the most vulnerable in connection with health professionals.
missions.transFull: Our primary mission is to accompany orphans in order to offer them protection and dignity. We also support women victims of violence as well as the most vulnerable people. Everyone benefits from attentive listening, emergency material aid and support towards social reintegration, provided in connection with health professionals. Human dignity remains the principle that guides every action of the association.

cta.title: Every gesture counts
cta.text: Join our mission and help us offer a smile and a future to those who need it most.

about.manifestoTitle: A mission born from the heart, carried by action
about.manifestoLead: The Mama Esther Association was born from a simple desire: to no longer remain a spectator in the face of the distress of orphans in Cameroon.
about.manifestoText: Since our creation, we have worked tirelessly to offer not only a roof and food, but above all an education and a dignified future to every child we meet.

integrity.title: Integrity
integrity.desc: Every cent is used for the cause.

commitment.title: Commitment
commitment.desc: A constant presence on the ground.

love.title: Love
love.desc: Placing humans at the heart of every project.

about.teamCtaText: Whether you are a volunteer, donor or simple sympathizer, your place is among us.

actuality.subscribeText: Receive our latest impact stories directly in your mailbox.

contact.infoTitle: Want to join us or ask a question?
contact.infoText: Whether you are an individual, a company or another association, every message counts for us.
contact.impactText: We act mainly in the Yaoundé and Douala regions in Cameroon.

don.heroTitle: Every donation is a promise of future
don.supportText: You can make a one-time donation or become a sponsor of a child for long-term support.
don.secureText: Your data is protected via HelloAsso, the leading platform in the associative sector.
don.taxText: Benefit from a tax reduction of 66% of the amount of your donation.
don.formFooter: You will be redirected to our secure payment partner.

transparencyModal.title: Responsible and transparent use of donations
transparencyModal.p1: As the association is still in the construction phase, an essential part of the donations is currently dedicated to the development of the infrastructure necessary for the reception and accompaniment of the beneficiaries. This priority ensures that our future actions can take place in a safe, sustainable and adapted framework for the needs of the people we support.
transparencyModal.p2: However, we sometimes intervene in emergencies when critical situations arise. In these cases, the available resources are mobilized to provide immediate and targeted aid to the most vulnerable people.
transparencyModal.p3: Transparency remains a fundamental principle of our operation. All expenses, actions taken and the progress of projects will be detailed in our annual report, and we remain available to provide any additional information upon request.

nutrition.title: Nutrition
nutrition.desc: 15€ provides balanced meals to a child for a month.

education.title: Education
education.desc: 50€ finances tuition fees and annual school supplies.

health.title: Health
health.desc: 100€ covers emergency medical care for the orphanage.

sponsor.heroTitle: Building the future of orphans together
sponsor.heroText: Becoming a partner of the Mama Esther Association means committing concretely to dignity and education in Cameroon.

manifesto.lead: Every day, Mama Esther meets eyes filled with hope.
manifesto.p1: Children who dream of going to school. Mothers who struggle to feed their families. Entire communities moving forward with courage, despite the lack of means.
manifesto.p2: Faced with these realities, we have chosen to act. Not with grand speeches, but with concrete gestures: a warm meal, a schoolbag, access to care, a repaired roof, training that changes a life. And today, we need new strength to continue.
manifesto.highlight: This is where your commitment can change everything.
manifesto.intro: By becoming a partner or sponsor of Mama Esther, you are not simply funding a project:
manifesto.list1: You offer a real chance to a child
manifesto.list2: You restore dignity to a family
manifesto.list3: You help build a fairer future
manifesto.p3: Every collaboration becomes a shared story. One outstretched hand meeting another. Proof that solidarity is not a concept, but a living energy capable of transforming lives.
manifesto.p4: We build sincere, transparent, and human partnerships. We move forward together, step by step, with clear monitoring, visible results, and mutual trust. Because behind every action, there are faces, smiles, and paths being reborn.
manifesto.call1: Joining Mama Esther means choosing to be on the side of light.
manifesto.call2: It's deciding that your company, your organization, or your personal commitment can become an engine of hope.
manifesto.footer: What if we wrote the rest together?

benefits.subtitle: Why join us?
benefits.title: Give meaning to your commitment
benefits.impactDesc: Every euro invested is tracked. We provide precise impact reports for your CSR communication.
benefits.taxDesc: As a recognized association, your donations entitle you to a tax reduction (60% for companies in France).
benefits.imageDesc: We highlight our partners on our materials (website, newsletters, social media).

types.materialDesc: School supplies, medical equipment, or construction materials.

fundraising.heroText: Because every object, every supply and every financial donation brings an orphan closer to their dream.
fundraising.materialText: We collect essential items for the daily life of children and the smooth running of the orphanage.

volunteer.heroText: Put your talent, your time and your heart at the service of a cause that changes lives daily.
volunteer.opportunitiesText: Whether you are in Europe or on the ground in Cameroon, your help is precious.

cta.title: Ready to be part of the team?
